2606.29776.title: Towards Generalizable and Evidential Nuclear Magnetic Resonance-Based Molecular Structure Elucidation via Large Language Model Agent
2606.29776.paper_summary: Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) spectroscopy is the gold standard for molecular structure elucidation, yet interpreting complex spectra for unknown molecules remains a bottleneck reliant on human expertise. While artificial intelligence has advanced this field, current methods face a critical trade-off: database retrieval cannot identify novel scaffolds, while de novo molecular structure elucidation models operate as black boxes, lacking the atom-level interpretability required for rigorous scientific validation. Here, we present NMRAgent, an evidential reasoning agent powered by large language models (LLMs) that bridges this gap by integrating specialized spectral analysis tools with chemical knowledge graphs. Unlike previous approaches, NMRAgent mimics the deductive reasoning of human experts: it takes experimental NMR spectra and molecular formula as input, plans the elucidation process, proposes candidate structures, verifies peak-atom consistency, and refines misaligned substructure through formula-aware fragment optimization. Enabled by its evidential reasoning, NMRAgent outperforms state-of-the-art methods, improving top-1 accuracy by 46.5% and Tanimoto similarity by 0.502 on a scaffold-split benchmark with novel scaffolds in the test set. Besides, we demonstrate the agent's practical utility by elucidating the structures of two previously unknown natural products isolated from Hydrangea davidii and Vitex trifolia, and by correcting structural misassignments in established literature. By combining high-accuracy prediction with transparent and evidence-based reasoning, NMRAgent establishes a new paradigm for interpretable AI in analytical chemistry.
2606.29776.paper_summary_zh: 核磁共振（NMR）光譜學是分子結構闡明的金標準，但對於未知分子的複雜光譜進行解釋仍然是一個依賴人類專業知識的瓶頸。儘管人工智慧已經推進了這一領域，但當前的方法面臨一個關鍵的權衡：數據庫檢索無法識別新穎的骨架，而從頭開始的分子結構闡明模型則作為黑箱運作，缺乏嚴謹科學驗證所需的原子級可解釋性。在此，我們介紹了NMRAgent，一個由大型語言模型（LLMs）驅動的證據推理代理，通過將專門的光譜分析工具與化學知識圖譜整合，彌補了這一空白。與以往的方法不同，NMRAgent模仿人類專家的演繹推理：它以實驗NMR光譜和分子式作為輸入，規劃闡明過程，提出候選結構，驗證峰-原子一致性，並通過考慮公式的片段優化來細化不對齊的子結構。憑藉其證據推理，NMRAgent超越了最先進的方法，在具有新穎骨架的測試集的骨架拆分基準上，將頂級準確率提高了46.5%，Tanimoto相似度提高了0.502。此外，我們通過闡明從大花繡球（Hydrangea davidii）和三葉青（Vitex trifolia）中分離出的兩種先前未知的天然產物的結構，以及糾正已建立文獻中的結構錯誤，展示了該代理的實用性。通過將高準確度預測與透明和基於證據的推理相結合，NMRAgent為分析化學中的可解釋AI建立了一個新的範式。
2606.29776.author: Zheng Fang et.al.
2606.29776.authors: Zheng Fang, Chen Yang, Yusen Tan, Yunpeng Zhao, Fanjie Xu, Hongxin Xiang, Hanyu Sun, Hanyu Gao, Xiaojian Wang, Wenjie Du, Yuqiang Li, Jun Xia
